summary: Pristine soundboard capture of the historic Duke April 24‑1971 show, highlighted by a smoking “Hard to Handle” and tight Not Fade Away → GDTRFB → NFA sequence.
review: The recording is an excellent soundboard mix – crisp, well‑separated and free of major flaws – that lets the band’s 1971 form shine through.  Performances are tight yet still full of the era’s loose charm, with Jerry’s guitar tone on “Hard to Handle” and “Sugar Magnolia”, Phil’s bass solid throughout the Not Fade Away → GDTRFB → NFA medley, and Pig‑Pen’s soulful contributions.  Reviewers repeatedly call this a must‑have April ’71 concert despite its relatively short second set.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (8 × 5★, 7 × 4★, 1 × 3★) praise both the musical performance and the soundboard quality; only a single modest‑score comment notes a “businesslike” vibe, so the overall rating is strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, pristine mix with excellent instrument separation; occasional random chatter but no noticeable drop‑outs or muddiness. Reviewers call it a “great sounding board”, “super 71’ soundboard” and note the flawless guitar tone.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert is praised for its tight, business‑like delivery with less sprawling jamming than later ’71 dates; the presence of multiple opening acts shortens the second set, but individual tunes are executed perfectly.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone described as pristine and “smoking”, with a notable wah‑wah on Sugar Magnolia and beautiful solo on Sing Me Back Home.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is highlighted as especially on point during the Not Fade Away → GDTRFB → NFA sequence, delivering tight, solid support.
